analysis.summary: The technician initiated a remote support session, reviewing network device configurations in PuTTY. A significant portion of the session involved troubleshooting access to Microsoft Word and Microsoft Teams, requiring multiple sign-in attempts and a Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) process to open a runbook document. After successfully downloading the document and dismissing a location prompt in Teams, the technician briefly inspected device drivers in Device Manager, focusing on network adapters and USB controllers.
actions_taken: Attempted to sign into Microsoft Word to open a document.
Searched for runbook documents using Windows Search.
Navigated to the file location of a runbook document within OneDrive/Microsoft Teams Chat Files.
Signed into Microsoft Teams, providing email and password.
Completed Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for Microsoft Teams via text message code.
Dismissed a location access prompt for Microsoft Teams.
Expanded and reviewed details under 'Network adapters' and 'Universal Serial Bus controllers' in Device Manager.
issues_observed: Microsoft Word prompted for sign-in to access the document.
Microsoft Teams required sign-in and Multi-Factor Authentication.
Initial delay/stall at 0% during a file download from Microsoft Teams.
Microsoft Teams prompted for location access permission.
issues_resolved: Successfully signed into Microsoft Teams and completed MFA.
Successfully downloaded a document via Microsoft Teams.
machines_touched: TE-HQ-LAB-02 (network device hostname visible in PuTTY and Word document)
VM-A-10.6.9.101/24 (IP configuration visible in Word document screenshot)
notable_events: Extended authentication process for Microsoft services involving both password entry and MFA.
A document from Microsoft Teams Chat Files showed a 'Protected View' warning.
File download from Teams was briefly stuck at 0% before completing.
concerns: The Word document displayed a 'Protected View' warning regarding potential viruses from internet files; proceeding with sign-in (potentially enabling editing) on such a document without explicit confirmation of its safety could pose a minor security risk, though the source (Teams Chat Files from Technijian, Inc) mitigates this.

tech_performance.efficiency_notes: The technician worked methodically to overcome authentication hurdles for Microsoft applications. The time spent was primarily due to the multi-step sign-in and MFA process, not a lack of clear direction. The navigation within Windows Search and Device Manager was also direct and purposeful.
